sections.paragraph:
(for. רְגֹב “a hear of stones,” from the root רָגַב=רָגַם) [Argob], pr.n. of a region situated beyond Jordan, in which were sixty cities, anciently subject to Og, king of Bashan, Deu 3:4, 13 1Ki 4:13. There is a mountain there now called Arkub Massalubie.

[(2) pr.n. of a man, 2Ki 15:25.]
